danger_signals.visual_form: Accumulated colored gems rising toward top of play area
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent loss condition; player is losing the match

danger_signals.visual_form: Reduction of playable vertical space in opponent's well
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent has spent points to reduce player advantage; active threat escalation

danger_signals.visual_form: Accelerated descent speed of gem stacks
danger_signals.meaning: Difficulty escalation; progression through game or match intensity increase

safety_signals.visual_form: Horizontal, vertical, or diagonal matches of three+ identical colored jewels disappearing
safety_signals.meaning: Successful move; player controlling board state

safety_signals.visual_form: Multicolor gem column appearing in play area
safety_signals.meaning: Power-up opportunity; potential to clear opponent's board or expand own space

safety_signals.visual_form: Increase of playable rows in player's well
safety_signals.meaning: Player gaining advantage through point expenditure
Access to board manipulation abilities unlocked

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: AI opponents themed as pyramid encounters (bats, mummies in single-player story mode)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Pyramid setting; Egyptian aesthetic in single-player progression

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Creature/monster theming (bats, mummies) with difficulty scaling
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Pyramid rooms; progression through different chambers with distinct opponent difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Slower column descent; ample board space; fewer jewels accumulated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited board manipulation options available; opponent cannot reduce player space

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Access to C-button board control visuals; Magic Jewels begin appearing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can spend points to reduce opponent's play area; strategic offensive actions become available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Accelerated column fall speed; denser gem configurations; smaller playable wells
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent applies board pressure; cascading clears become critical; Magic Jewel usage escalates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponent's well expanding; player's well shrinking; gem pile rising in player's area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Loss condition approaching; player forced to use continues (Hourglass items) to rematch

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pyramid room transitions; increasing visual complexity of opponent theming
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty escalation through difficulty select (easy/medium/hard); progressively harder AI opponents; greater jewel accumulation rates

environmental_storytelling.example: Single-player story mode encounters themed as 'pyramid rooms' with creature opponents (bats, mummies) to establish Egyptian adventure narrative within puzzle competition framework
Easy and medium difficulty modes restrict level access, implying player advancement through pyramid structure; hard mode unlocks full level span

environmental_storytelling.visual_manifestation: Dual well display with asymmetrical board sizes; board manipulation mechanics (shrinking/expanding) create visual metaphor of dominance/submission between competitors

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Multicolor configuration; triggers board-wide effects (opponent space reduction, player space recovery, color-specific mass deletion) when landing
Slower response; minimal board manipulation; predictable gem placement patterns
Accelerated column management; strategic Magic Jewel usage; aggressive board compression on player
Responds to player input (directional movement, symbol rotation); falls at speed dependent on game progression
Matched symbols disappear; remaining gems settle under gravity; cascading matches trigger if new alignments form

notes: Columns III is a competitive puzzle game with minimal narrative framing. Visual communication relies entirely on board state representation (gem colors, well dimensions, score thresholds) and procedural difficulty signaling (fall speed, opponent AI behavior). The single-player Egyptian pyramid setting provides weak environmental storytelling but does not functionally affect game mechanics. Faction distinction is implicit (player vs. AI), with no explicit visual faction identity markers. World state is communicated through dynamic board parameters (well size, gem density, fall velocity) rather than atmospheric or narrative shifts. The game prioritizes mechanical clarity over worldbuilding.
